topic: A student discovers their teacher follows them on social media
raw_output:
[story 1]

The blue light of my phone felt like a physical weight in my palm. I scrolled through my feed, a familiar ache settling in my chest with every notification. Then I saw it: a post from Mr. Harrison. A photo of a slightly blurry campus landmark, captioned with a cryptic, almost mocking observation about student life. My breath hitched. I hadn't seen him in person all week, yet here he was, lurking in my digital space. A tightness gripped my throat, making it hard to swallow. I quickly minimized the app, my fingers trembling slightly as I tried to regain composure. The silence of my room suddenly amplified, each tick of the clock sounding loud and intrusive.

[story 2]

I stared at the screen, my jaw tight. It was an image of a textbook, clearly taken from a class assignment, but overlaid with a filter that made it look strangely dramatic. Mr. Harrison’s handle was right there. A slow, cold dread began to pool in my stomach. I reread the caption, the words seeming sharper now, laced with an unintended implication. I felt a sudden urge to slam the device down, to shatter the glass, but my hand remained frozen above the surface. My shoulders slumped, a heavy surrender to the unwelcome intrusion. The air in the room seemed to thicken, making it difficult to draw a full breath.

[story 3]

It started innocently enough—a shared class group chat that had suddenly become a conduit for something entirely different. I clicked on Mr. Harrison’s profile, expecting to see syllabus updates. Instead, I found a series of carefully curated snapshots of his weekend activities. A small, almost imperceptible tremor ran through my hands as I scrolled through them. My gaze snagged on a picture taken near the library entrance, and a profound sense of isolation washed over me. I pulled my knees to my chest, wrapping my arms around them tightly. The world outside the screen seemed distant and muted, leaving me stranded in a quiet, uncomfortable space.
